mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and credit availability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, with a mean return difference of less than 0.1% versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3.0 percent annually while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5 percent, the resulting stagflationary pressure compresses corporate margins and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive forward returns in over 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals sustained economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annualized indicates a sharp deceleration in aggregate economic activity, which reliably precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth below the threshold fails to predict negative returns, with forward returns remaining positive in >70% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and a rise in the unemployment rate confirms a broad-based economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The co-movement of declining INDPRO and rising UNRATE does not yield a statistically significant negative return differential (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
